adjudication_raw:
Review: Clinical sepsis prediction evaluation.

Verdict: critique_wins

The AUROC comparison is methodologically compromised by three design asymmetries: (1) the ML model's best prediction over 6 hours vs. qSOFA at a single triage point — a window-selection advantage for the ML model; (2) post-triage laboratory features (lactate, WBC) included in ML model during the window evaluation, unavailable at qSOFA's comparison point; (3) surveillance deployment claim vs. triage-point baseline — comparing fundamentally different clinical use cases. Each issue independently inflates the 0.15 AUROC gap. The paper should not support a prospective trial request without a redesigned evaluation using matched time windows and a surveillance-appropriate baseline.
